heading: Date when Tax Court decision becomes final
label: 7481 Date when Tax Court decision becomes final
notes: Amendments1997—Subsec. (b). , substituted “section 7436(c) or 7463” for “section 7463”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1454(b)(3)Subsec. (c). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Notwithstanding subsection (a), if—Pub. L. 105–34, § 1452(a)“(1) an assessment has been made by the Secretary under section 6215 which includes interest as imposed by this title,“(2) the taxpayer has paid the entire amount of the deficiency plus interest claimed by the Secretary, and“(3) within 1 year after the date the decision of the Tax Court becomes final under subsection (a), the taxpayer files a petition in the Tax Court for a determination that the amount of interest claimed by the Secretary exceeds the amount of interest imposed by this title,then the Tax Court may reopen the case solely to determine whether the taxpayer has made an overpayment of such interest and the amount of any such overpayment. If the Tax Court determines under this subsection that the taxpayer has made an overpayment of interest, then that determination shall be treated under section 6512(b)(1) as a determination of an overpayment of tax. An order of the Tax Court redetermining the interest due, when entered upon the records of the court, shall be reviewable in the same manner as a decision of the Tax Court.”1988—Subsec. (a). , substituted “subsections (b), (c), and (d)” for “subsections (b) and (c)”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 6247(b)(2), substituted “subsections (b) and (c)” for “subsection (b)”.Pub. children.heading: Timely notice of appeal not filed
children.label: 1 Timely notice of appeal not filed
children.text: Upon the expiration of the time allowed for filing a notice of appeal, if no such notice has been duly filed within such time; or

children.heading: Petition for certiorari not filed on time
children.label: A Petition for certiorari not filed on time
children.text: Upon the expiration of the time allowed for filing a petition for certiorari, if the decision of the Tax Court has been affirmed or the appeal dismissed by the United States Court of Appeals and no petition for certiorari has been duly filed; or

children.heading: Petition for certiorari denied
children.label: B Petition for certiorari denied
children.text: Upon the denial of a petition for certiorari, if the decision of the Tax Court has been affirmed or the appeal dismissed by the United States Court of Appeals; or

children.heading: After mandate of Supreme Court
children.label: C After mandate of Supreme Court
children.text: Upon the expiration of 30 days from the date of issuance of the mandate of the Supreme Court, if such Court directs that the decision of the Tax Court be affirmed or the appeal dismissed.

children.heading: Upon mandate of Supreme Court
children.label: A Upon mandate of Supreme Court
children.text: If the Supreme Court directs that the decision of the Tax Court be modified or reversed, the decision of the Tax Court rendered in accordance with the mandate of the Supreme Court shall become final upon the expiration of 30 days from the time it was rendered, unless within such 30 days either the Secretary or the taxpayer has instituted proceedings to have such decision corrected to accord with the mandate, in which event the decision of the Tax Court shall become final when so corrected.

children.heading: Upon mandate of the Court of Appeals
children.label: B Upon mandate of the Court of Appeals
i
children.text: the time allowed for filing a petition for certiorari has expired and no such petition has been duly filed, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the petition for certiorari has been denied, or

children.label: iii
children.text: the decision of the United States Court of Appeals has been affirmed by the Supreme Court, then the decision of the Tax Court rendered in accordance with the mandate of the United States Court of Appeals shall become final on the expiration of 30 days from the time such decision of the Tax Court was rendered, unless within such 30 days either the Secretary or the taxpayer has instituted proceedings to have such decision corrected so that it will accord with the mandate, in which event the decision of the Tax Court shall become final when so corrected.

children.label: A
children.text: the time allowed for filing a petition for certiorari has expired and no such petition has been duly filed, or

children.label: B
children.text: the petition for certiorari has been denied, or

children.label: C
children.text: the decision of the United States Court of Appeals has been affirmed by the Supreme Court,

children.heading: Definition of “mandate”
children.label: 5 Definition of “mandate”
children.text: As used in this section, the term “mandate”, in case a mandate has been recalled prior to the expiration of 30 days from the date of issuance thereof, means the final mandate.

children.heading: Nonreviewable decisions
children.label: b Nonreviewable decisions
children.text: The decision of the Tax Court in a proceeding conducted under section 7436(c) or 7463 shall become final upon the expiration of 90 days after the decision is entered.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), if, within 1 year after the date the decision of the Tax Court becomes final under subsection (a) in a case to which this subsection applies, the taxpayer files a motion in the Tax Court for a redetermination of the amount of interest involved, then the Tax Court may reopen the case solely to determine whether the taxpayer has made an overpayment of such interest or the Secretary has made an underpayment of such interest and the amount thereof.

children.heading: Cases to which this subsection applies
children.label: 2 Cases to which this subsection applies
i
children.text: an assessment has been made by the Secretary under section 6215 which includes interest as imposed by this title, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the taxpayer has paid the entire amount of the deficiency plus interest claimed by the Secretary, and

children.label: B
children.text: the Tax Court finds under section 6512(b) that the taxpayer has made an overpayment.

children.heading: Special rules
children.label: 3 Special rules
children.text: If the Tax Court determines under this subsection that the taxpayer has made an overpayment of interest or that the Secretary has made an underpayment of interest, then that determination shall be treated under section 6512(b)(1) as a determination of an overpayment of tax. An order of the Tax Court redetermining interest, when entered upon the records of the court, shall be reviewable in the same manner as a decision of the Tax Court.

children.heading: Decisions relating to estate tax extended under section 6166
children.label: d Decisions relating to estate tax extended under section 6166

children.text: the time for payment of an amount of tax imposed by chapter 11 is extended under section 6166, and

children.label: A
children.text: any amount of interest which a decedent’s estate pays on any portion of the tax imposed by section 2001 on such estate for which the time of payment is extended under section 6166, or

children.label: B
children.text: interest on any estate, succession, legacy, or inheritance tax imposed by a State on such estate during the period of the extension of time for payment under section 6166,
